title: Please Homer Dont Hammer Em

jokes.text: Yeah, hasn't been the same since they murdered the Mayor's dad here.
jokes.setup: Comment about a place looking run down
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of murder as explanation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly, good economy and structure

jokes.text: Now it's a collection of candle kiosks and Persian CD stores
jokes.setup: J.C. Penney's used to be here
jokes.punchline: Replaced by oddly specific cultural stereotype collection
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about mall decline with specific details

jokes.text: Is like Bruce Springsteen the Boss, ya?
jokes.setup: Trying to sell Persian music
jokes.punchline: Comparing obscure Persian artist to Springsteen
jokes.explanation: Good character-based absurdity with cultural disconnect humor

jokes.text: If you buy now, I give you, uh, toothache cream. / Deal!
jokes.setup: Homer considering buying Persian CDs
jokes.punchline: Random toothache cream as deal sweetener that Homer accepts
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation with perfect Homer characterization - he'd accept any random add-on

jokes.text: These games are so old. Who got these high scores? Pilgrims?
jokes.setup: Looking at old arcade games
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated age reference to Pilgrims
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons hyperbolic comparison, very quotable structure

jokes.text: Now my ship is pooping more triangles.
jokes.setup: Homer confused by arcade game mechanics
jokes.punchline: Childish 'pooping' description of game spawning objects
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike interpretation fits character perfectly, mildly crude but earned

jokes.text: Don't tell me how many times to think!
jokes.setup: Marge suggests thinking twice about old candy
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal/absurd interpretation of the phrase
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - takes common phrase literally in perfectly stupid way. Excellent character integration and economy.

jokes.text: This doesn't taste like I thought it would.
jokes.setup: Homer eating very old gummy worms
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about disgusting old candy
jokes.explanation: Functional but predictable gross-out humor, basic understatement

jokes.text: We already own a book.
jokes.setup: Marge suggests checking out discount book warehouse
jokes.punchline: Homer's anti-intellectual logic about book ownership
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer anti-intellectualism delivered with great economy and character truth

jokes.text: Redheaded twins? Their mother must have her hands full.
jokes.setup: Looking at book called 'Smiles of Ireland'
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent misinterpretation of suggestive content
jokes.explanation: Good character-based innocence humor - Marge missing the adult implication

jokes.text: This doesn't look easy, but I'll bet it is.
jokes.setup: Homer looking at carpentry book
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards logic about difficulty
jokes.explanation: Solid Homer overconfidence joke, sets up later carpentry plot

jokes.text: Blah, blah, blah... Okay, now you do me.
jokes.setup: Homer pretending to read to Marge
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's just saying 'blah' and expects reciprocation
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag revealing Homer's fake reading, good character consistency

jokes.text: There were eels in the photo booth.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining why she doesn't want to return to mall
jokes.punchline: Completely random and disgusting detail
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about mall decay, very quotable randomness

jokes.text: A fire? I didn't start a fire in the teachers' lounge. I mean, what fire?
jokes.setup: Bart receiving letter from school
jokes.punchline: Bart's guilty overreaction reveals he did start a fire
jokes.explanation: Classic guilty overreaction structure, perfect Bart characterization

jokes.text: Good grief, more peanuts!
jokes.setup: Finding peanuts in Bart's lunch after peanut ban
jokes.punchline: Charlie Brown reference plus escalating peanut discoveries
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference with escalation, but fairly standard

jokes.text: And the Pecker Brothers, Danny and Dell!
jokes.setup: Announcing NASCAR-style crash victims
jokes.punchline: Crude double entendre name in context of violence
jokes.explanation: Juvenile wordplay, somewhat cheap humor relying on crude name

jokes.text: We are worse than the ancient Romans!
jokes.setup: Watching violent derby coverage
jokes.punchline: Homer's sudden moral clarity and historical comparison
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of Homer having moral insight while watching violence

jokes.text: What's the point? We're all slowly dying.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about follow-through on projects
jokes.punchline: Homer's nihilistic response to carpentry question
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor escalation, perfectly timed existential dread from Homer

jokes.text: What's that strange feeling? It's 'of accomplishment'!
jokes.setup: Homer successfully fixing nightstand
jokes.punchline: Homer unfamiliar with basic positive emotion
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Homer so lazy he doesn't recognize accomplishment

jokes.text: It's like you're the Jesus of carpentry.
jokes.setup: Bart impressed by Marge's carpentry
jokes.punchline: Religious comparison ignoring Jesus was literally a carpenter
jokes.explanation: Clever irony - comparing someone to Jesus for carpentry when Jesus was actually a carpenter

jokes.text: Aww, what a sweet blasphemy.
jokes.setup: Bart's Jesus comparison
jokes.punchline: Marge's gentle acknowledgment of the irreverence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - acknowledging blasphemy while finding it endearing. Excellent character voice.

jokes.text: I'm already arranging the clip art for the letterhead in my mind.
jokes.setup: Marge considering carpentry business
jokes.punchline: Very specific, mundane detail about business planning
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Marge's practical, domestic mindset

jokes.text: I have some pictures hanging that you might find, um... unsavory.
jokes.setup: Client hesitant about female carpenter
jokes.punchline: Awkward assumption about woman's sensibilities regarding porn
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe humor about sexist assumptions and awkwardness

jokes.text: What if you get pregnant and I'm left with half a hot tub? And don't tell me you're infertile. I ain't falling for that again.
jokes.setup: Client's sexist concerns about female carpenter
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd personal details and past experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with dark implication he's been 'fooled' by infertility claims before

jokes.text: I guess people just expect their carpenter to be some fat guy with his butt crack showing.
jokes.setup: Marge frustrated about gender discrimination
jokes.punchline: Specific stereotype description that perfectly describes Homer
jokes.explanation: Great setup for visual callback - Marge unknowingly describing Homer perfectly

jokes.text: Homer, your butt just gave me a brilliant idea!
jokes.setup: Marge's previous carpenter stereotype description
jokes.punchline: Homer's exposed butt crack inspiring the solution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual callback - Homer literally embodies the stereotype Marge just described, perfect plot mechanism

jokes.text: Why don't you go inside and stir your flubber while I get to work?
jokes.setup: Homer talking to elderly client
jokes.punchline: Homer's assumption about what old scientists do
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Homer's pop culture-based worldview

jokes.text: I thought I heard the sound of a woman measuring.
jokes.setup: Client catching Marge working
jokes.punchline: Absurd idea that women measure differently/audibly
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist sexism - the idea that gender affects measuring sounds

jokes.text: Measure like a girl, hammer like a guy.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to cover for Marge's presence
jokes.punchline: Making up fake sayings to explain the situation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer improvisation - creating fake wisdom to cover his lie

jokes.text: Hammer with the wood and it's all good.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to make up construction sayings
jokes.punchline: Even more nonsensical fake wisdom with forced rhyme
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of Homer's improvised fake construction wisdom

jokes.text: A dash of glue, a turn of screw, and a gazebo's built that's perfect for two... or more.
jokes.setup: Marge narrating her construction work
jokes.punchline: Innocent rhyme with unintended sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Marge's innocent pride with unintended innuendo

jokes.text: I'd have this gazebo torn down and built into a coffin... for your manhood.
jokes.setup: Homer asking hypothetically about woman doing the work
jokes.punchline: Extreme overreaction with dramatic metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with creative metaphor about masculinity, very quotable

jokes.text: Like all manly men, I have a vivid imagination.
jokes.setup: Client asking why Homer's hypothetical scared him
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards logic about masculinity and imagination
jokes.explanation: Great Homer logic - connecting vivid imagination to manliness in absurd way

jokes.text: Let's take off our shirts and wrestle.
jokes.setup: Client praising Homer's manly explanation
jokes.punchline: Sudden homoerotic escalation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation, undermines the 'manly' posturing with homoerotic suggestion

jokes.text: Oh, why should we have to give up our salty snacks just to keep one allergic kid from exploding?
jokes.setup: Kids complaining about peanut ban
jokes.punchline: Callous concern for snacks over child's life, dramatic 'exploding'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson characterization - selfish and dramatically callous about others' safety

jokes.text: I'm only allergic to honey, wheat, dairy, non-dairy, and my own tears.
jokes.setup: Milhouse denying he's the peanut-allergic kid
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous list of allergies ending with impossible one
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect Milhouse characterization - pathetic and physically fragile

jokes.text: A principal can be a man or a woman these days.
jokes.setup: Willie accidentally revealing the allergic person is male
jokes.punchline: Trying to cover with progressive gender commentary
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection attempt by Willie, funny contrast with his usual character

jokes.text: Just like the English took our sheep and our women in 1291. Then, they sent them back, which was worse!
jokes.setup: Willie comparing peanut confiscation to historical oppression
jokes.punchline: Escalating to implication that Scottish women/sheep were returned as damaged goods
jokes.explanation: Excellent Willie rant with perfect character voice, dark escalation that's historically absurd

jokes.text: My allergy is so sensitive, a single molecule would make my throat close like a museum at 4:45.
jokes.setup: Skinner warning about his peanut allergy severity
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and bureaucratic metaphor for throat closing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Skinner line - perfectly captures his bureaucratic mindset even when describing medical emergency. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: You provide food and sexual release for your handy husband.
jokes.setup: Helen dismissing Marge's contribution to Homer's business
jokes.punchline: Crude reduction of Marge's role to basic services
jokes.explanation: Good Helen characterization - passive-aggressive cruelty disguised as matter-of-fact observation

jokes.text: I sat on it so much it's a little sore.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Marge to rub his butt
jokes.punchline: Explaining soreness from excessive sitting/laziness
jokes.explanation: Basic Homer laziness joke, fits character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You'll be married to some kind of gay jelly, floating outside the Florida Keys, cruising for rich snorkelers.
jokes.setup: Homer warning about consequences of being exposed as fraud
jokes.punchline: Bizarre jellyfish metaphor for emasculation
jokes.explanation: Incredibly creative absurdist metaphor from Homer - jellyfish as symbol of emasculation is brilliantly weird

jokes.text: Yes, you might say he'll 'blow up.'
jokes.setup: Lisa saying Skinner will be mad at Bart
jokes.punchline: Double meaning with Skinner's peanut allergy
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay connecting Skinner's anger with his allergic reaction potential

jokes.text: You might say it drives me nuts.
jokes.setup: Bart saying he doesn't like detention
jokes.punchline: Pun on nuts while carrying peanuts that will trigger allergy
jokes.explanation: Decent setup for the peanut catastrophe, basic pun but contextually appropriate

jokes.text: Maybe finally shut him up about the high price of cornmeal.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Lenny wants a windmill
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific complaint about Lenny's cornmeal grievances
jokes.explanation: Good specific character detail - Lenny as someone who complains about cornmeal prices

jokes.text: Who do you think would help me, my wife?
jokes.setup: Carl praising Homer for building windmill with his own hands
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically dismissing idea his wife would help
jokes.explanation: Great ironic moment - Homer dismissing the exact truth while taking credit for Marge's work

jokes.text: The only thing women can build is credit card debt.
jokes.setup: Carl responding to Homer's dismissal of wife helping
jokes.punchline: Sexist stereotype delivered as matter-of-fact observation
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexist joke, relies on stereotype without much craft or originality

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, infinity.
jokes.setup: Marge apologizing for cabinet disaster
jokes.punchline: Childlike escalation to mathematical infinity
jokes.explanation: Good Marge characterization - escalating apology in innocent, mathematical way

jokes.text: We need to replace them with kick-ass going-down parts.
jokes.setup: Homer analyzing roller coaster problems
jokes.punchline: Oversimplified solution focusing only on excitement
jokes.explanation: Good Homer engineering philosophy - ignoring safety for maximum thrills

jokes.text: What are struts?! Oh, I don't know anything about struts.
jokes.setup: Worker asking about securing struts
jokes.punchline: Homer's panic revealing complete ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's complete incompetence under pressure

jokes.text: This guy's all bubble and no level.
jokes.setup: Worker observing Homer's incompetence
jokes.punchline: Construction tool pun about being fake/unbalanced
jokes.explanation: Excellent construction-specific wordplay - 'all bubble and no level' is perfect tool-based metaphor

jokes.text: I'm supposed to stand in your store window and breast-feed Bilbo Baggins.
jokes.setup: Woman entering comic book store
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific request combining fantasy and inappropriate activity
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and specific - random character with incredibly weird request that's highly quotable

jokes.text: Your cowering suggests that Bart has found your kryptonite.
jokes.setup: CBG observing Skinner's behavior around Bart
jokes.punchline: Comic book reference to explain the power dynamic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy dialogue - applying superhero logic to real situations with his typical superiority.

jokes.text: The 'ite' suffix suggests a mineral.
jokes.setup: Skinner asking what kryptonite is
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's pedantic linguistic analysis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy response - pedantic and condescending linguistic analysis

jokes.text: Perhaps. But for now, you may suckle your Baggins.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy responding to breast-feeding request
jokes.punchline: Formal dismissal treating bizarre request as normal business
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy response treating absurdity with characteristic pompous formality.

jokes.text: To Daddy, the real world gets fainter and fainter every day.
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting daddy who lives in real world
jokes.punchline: Homer's poetic admission of disconnection from reality
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly poetic and dark Homer moment - philosophical about his detachment from reality

jokes.text: Simpson, you've been waving your nuts in my face for too long.
jokes.setup: Skinner confronting Bart about peanuts
jokes.punchline: Unintended sexual double entendre about nuts
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre with Skinner's unintended sexual phrasing about peanuts

jokes.text: I... was... El Barto.
jokes.setup: Bart and Skinner falling into pit
jokes.punchline: Deathbed confession of graffiti identity
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic callback - revealing long-running series mystery in death scene parody

jokes.text: you won't get married on The Pharaoh's Curse.
jokes.setup: Man listing roller coasters girlfriend won't marry on
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate roller coaster names
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of roller coaster names but fairly standard background joke

jokes.text: that I'm perfect and everything I touch is perfect!
jokes.setup: Homer about to tell the truth
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on lies with grandiose claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer escalation - when caught lying, he lies bigger and more dramatically

jokes.text: He'll never hear me say 'I told you so!'
jokes.setup: Marge worried Homer will kill himself
jokes.punchline: Her main concern is missing chance to gloat
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge moment - her priorities hilariously skewed toward vindication over safety

jokes.text: Avenge... me.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently dying under roller coaster wreckage
jokes.punchline: Dramatic last words that are completely inappropriate to situation
jokes.explanation: Good parody of dramatic death scenes with Homer's inappropriate final request

tmdb_overview: Marge reads Homer's carpentry books and becomes very handy at fixing things. Gender bias keeps the townspeople from accepting a female carpenter, so Marge pretends that Homer does the work. Meanwhile, Bart torments Skinner when he learns the principal has an extreme allergy to peanuts.
